title: Public sentiment regarding the return to service of the Boeing 737 MAX aircraft following the 2019 grounding

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on post-crash sentiment nadir in early 2020
Estimated: Gradual recovery as grounding extended
Estimated: Impact of initial recertification flight testing news
Estimated: Increased confidence following FAA flight tests

events.description: FAA and Boeing begin formal certification flight tests
FAA Administrator Steve Dickson conducts his own evaluation flight
FAA officially clears the Boeing 737 MAX to return to commercial service

agents.name: Boeing Corporate
agents.description: Manufacturer seeking to restore brand reputation and market share

agents.name: FAA
agents.description: Regulatory body responsible for safety certification

agents.name: Airline Pilots Association
agents.description: Labor union focused on pilot training and safety protocols

agents.name: Frequent Flyers
agents.description: Business travelers prioritizing schedule and availability

agents.name: Casual Travelers
agents.description: Leisure travelers highly sensitive to safety news and media coverage

agents.name: Aviation Safety Advocates
agents.description: Watchdog groups and families of crash victims

notes: Pro-side defined as passengers willing to book a flight on a 737 MAX. Against-side defined as passengers who would avoid the aircraft or are unsure/unwilling. Data quality is high for the final round but relies on linear interpolation/estimates for earlier rounds based on the trajectory of regulatory news.
